steps.title: Map the passage structure
steps.content:
The passage opens with three broad sentences establishing a general situation: many early films are lost, including those by the first wave of Black women filmmakers, and we know about them only from small pieces of evidence. Everything after “For example” provides specific instances of that evidence (an advertisement, a magazine reference, a letter, a newspaper article, and a discovered image).

The structural signal “For example” is the key: it announces that concrete specifics follow a general claim.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The text identifies a complex problem, then presents examples of unsuccessful attempts to solve that problem.’: The situation is not framed as a “complex problem,” and the examples (an ad, a reference, a letter) are not attempts to solve anything — they are surviving evidence. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The text summarizes a debate among researchers, then gives reasons for supporting one side in that debate.’: No debate or competing scholarly positions appear anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The text describes a general situation, then illustrates that situation with specific examples.’: Matches exactly: the general situation (films are lost, only fragments of evidence survive) is followed by three specific examples of that evidence. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘The text discusses several notable individuals, then explains commonly overlooked differences between those individuals.’: The filmmakers are named only as examples of lost films; the passage does not compare or contrast the individuals themselves. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
